void.$description: Void — Neutral form states, badge text, and quiet UI feedback where color should stay understated. Do not promote it to the primary CTA color

bone.$description: Bone — Primary text, heading strokes, nav links, icon strokes, badge fills, logo type — the only high-contrast value in the system

soot.$description: Soot — Subtle tonal break from pure black for nested surfaces and hairline borders that need to feel present without breaking the dark mode

ember-coral.$description: Ember Coral — Accent warmth for marquee display moments, logo gradient highlight, and select project card color treatments — borrowed from the work, never applied to UI chrome

burnt-sienna.$description: Burnt Sienna — Deep warm surface for warm-toned project cards and content imagery containers — the shadow side of the coral accent

helvetica.$description: Primary workhorse — body copy at 16px/400, secondary headings at 23px/400, nav and links at 16px/400, and the massive 52px/300 hero logo treatment. Weight 300 is the default for large display: whisper-thin against the heavy black canvas creates a neon-on-velvet tension.

times-new-roman.$description: Display serif for editorial pull-quotes and manifesto text — the 60px size with -0.05em tracking creates a fashion-magazine weight that contrasts Helvetica's geometric neutrality. Used sparingly for sentences that need gravitas.

courier-new.$description: Monospace for tags, badges, index numbers, and micro-labels — the 0.10em positive tracking turns utility text into graphic texture, like film slate markings

void.$description: Surface level 0: Universal page canvas — every section sits on absolute black

soot.$description: Surface level 1: Nested containers and form fields needing barely-visible separation from canvas
